title: Defiled by hatred, brahmin, overcome, mind-possessed, one thinks of harming oneself, thinks of harming others, thinks of harming both, and experiences mental suffering and distress.…
content: Defiled by hatred, brahmin, overcome, mind-possessed, one thinks of harming oneself, thinks of harming others, thinks of harming both, and experiences mental suffering and distress. When hatred is abandoned, one does not think of harming oneself, does not think of harming others, does not think of harming both, and does not experience mental suffering and distress. Thus, brahmin, the Dhamma is directly visible, timeless, inviting investigation, onward-leading, to be personally experienced by the wise.

title: Bhikkhus, walk within your own proper range, within the territory of your ancestors. Bhikkhus, if you walk within your own proper range, within the territory…
content: Bhikkhus, walk within your own proper range, within the territory of your ancestors. Bhikkhus, if you walk within your own proper range, within the territory of your ancestors, then long life will increase, beauty will increase, happiness will increase, wealth will increase, power will increase.

title: Brahmin, I have practiced for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, I have established many people in the noble truth,…
content: Brahmin, I have practiced for the welfare of the many, for the happiness of the many, I have established many people in the noble truth, namely, in the nature of goodness, the nature of the good Dhamma. I am one who, regarding whatever thought I think should be thought, I think that thought; regarding whatever thought I think should not be thought, I do not think that thought. Regarding whatever intention I intend should be intended, I intend that intention; regarding whatever intention I intend should not be intended, I do not intend that intention. Brahmin, I have attained mastery of mind regarding the directions of thought. Brahmin, regarding the four jhānas that are higher states of mind, pleasant abidings in this very life, I obtain them without difficulty, without trouble, without effort. Brahmin, by the destruction of the taints, in this very life, with direct knowledge, I realize, attain, and abide in the taintless liberation of mind and liberation by wisdom.

title: I do not see any other single factor, bhikkhus, by which unarisen unwholesome states arise and arisen wholesome states are abandoned, as adherence to unwholesome…
content: I do not see any other single factor, bhikkhus, by which unarisen unwholesome states arise and arisen wholesome states are abandoned, as adherence to unwholesome states and non-adherence to wholesome states. Due to adherence to unwholesome states and non-adherence to wholesome states, bhikkhus, unarisen unwholesome states arise and arisen wholesome states are abandoned.

title: Mind precedes all phenomena, Mind is their master, mind is their maker. If one speaks or acts with a corrupted mind, Suffering follows, Like the…
content:
Mind precedes all phenomena,
Mind is their master, mind is their maker.
If one speaks or acts with a corrupted mind,
Suffering follows,
Like the wheel follows the ox's foot.

title: Samaṇa Gotama abstains from false speech, avoids false speech, speaks truth, adheres to truth, is reliable, trustworthy, does not deceive, does not break his word…
content: Samaṇa Gotama abstains from false speech, avoids false speech, speaks truth, adheres to truth, is reliable, trustworthy, does not deceive, does not break his word to the world. Samaṇa Gotama abstains from divisive speech, avoids divisive speech; what he hears here he does not repeat elsewhere to cause division among these people; what he hears elsewhere he does not repeat to these people to cause division among those people.

title: And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu called an Arahant? He has made evil, unwholesome states that are defiled, leading to renewed existence, fearful, yielding painful…
content: And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu called an Arahant? He has made evil, unwholesome states that are defiled, leading to renewed existence, fearful, yielding painful results, leading to future birth, aging, and death, far from him. Thus, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is an Arahant.

title: Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus delight in secluded dwellings, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.
content: Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus delight in secluded dwellings, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.

title: Standing to one side, the devata Khemā spoke this verse before the Blessed One: Fools without wisdom Treat the self as an enemy, They do…
content:
Standing to one side, the devata Khemā spoke this verse before the Blessed One:

Fools without wisdom
Treat the self as an enemy,
They do evil deeds
That bring bitter fruit.
A deed not well done,
Once done, torments one,
With tearful face,
Weeping, one experiences its result.
A deed well done,
Once done, does not torment one,
With joyful mind, at ease,
That one experiences its result.
Knowing what is good for oneself,
One should do what should be done first,
Not with the mind of a charioteer,
The wise one's mind is energetic.
Like a charioteer
Leaving the even highway,
Ascending a rough road,
Grieves over a broken axle.
So too, abandoning the Dhamma,
The fool follows non-Dhamma,
Falls into the mouth of Death,
Grieves like a broken axle.

title: Having given up children and spouse, Father and mother, money and grain, Relatives and the bonds of sensual desire, Live alone like a rhinoceros. Knowing…
content:
Having given up children and spouse,
Father and mother, money and grain,
Relatives and the bonds of sensual desire,
Live alone like a rhinoceros.

Knowing that this is bondage, that the happiness is slight,
That the gratification is little, and the suffering far greater,
This is a hook; knowing this, the wise person,
Live alone like a rhinoceros.
